title: lithocarpus spicatus var. placentarius
intro: Lithocarpus spicatus var. placentarius, also known as the Bur Oak or Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Lithocarpus spicatus var. placentarius include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties, and is often consumed in the form of tea or taken as a supplement.
